course_description:
This level 1 introductory course provides a comprehensive overview of the core concepts in biomedical science. Students will explore the fundamentals of human anatomy, physiology, and cell biology. The course consists of interactive workshops and lectures.

BIOM1001 (Fundamentals of Biomedical Sciences I) is a first-year biology course that provides a fundamental understanding of key concepts in biomedical sciences, including physiology and anatomy of human biological systems. Throughout the course, students will also explore major challenges in biomedical science and examine how the discipline contributes to advancing health, knowledge, and society. Delivered by research-active and teaching-focused academics from the School of Biomedical Sciences (SBMS), this course provides Bachelor of Biomedical Science students with an immersive introduction to biomedical science and a distinctive cohort experience as you begin your studies.

The course comprises four, three-week Modules, each covering a biological theme or system : Biomedical science practice, Nervous system, Cardiovascular system, and the Respiratory system. The spare week in the semester has been allocated as a co-curricular program to strengthen cohort connections and your sense of belonging to the biomedical science program. During this week, you will have the opportunity for meaningful engagement with your peers, students in higher levels of study, alumni, your teachers, and student societies through structured activities.

Course contact hours include one Lecture (2 hours) and one Workshop (2 hours) most weeks. In Workshops , you will explore and apply core concepts from lectures, through interactive, collaborative activities with peers, tutors and academics. Every third week, the workshop will be replaced with a module exam, except for Module 4, where the exam will be held during the end-of-semester period. Workshop activities are designed to cultivate your deep and critical thinking about biomedical science concepts, and prepare you for these module exams.

BIOM1001 is a 2-unit course. In addition to formal class contact, you are expected to engage in group and individual study progressively throughout semester, and be prepared to spend approximately 10-12 hours each week on course-related activities.

Attending all lectures, workshops and co-curricular activities is crucial to help you perform well in the course, and cultivate strong connections within your cohort.

course_aims:
BIOM1001 is structured around a set of overarching course aims that span all modules, alongside module-specific learning outcomes that relate to particular body systems and themes.

Overarching Course Aims:

learning_outcomes.description: Explain how scientific knowledge and progress is developed in biomedical science.
Identify and explain how the structure and function of the nervous system coordinate the body's response to internal and external stimuli.
Identify and explain how the anatomical structures of the heart and blood vessels relate to their physiological functions.
Identify and explain the relationships between the anatomical features of the respiratory system and its physiological functions.
Work and learn collaboratively in group settings, valuing and responding respectfully to diverse abilities, backgrounds, and perspectives.
Evaluate and reflect on your learning experiences throughout the course to identify strengths and areas for development in your understanding, learning strategies, and collaborative practice.

assessment_summary.weight: 12% 2% per quiz - best 6 scores out of 8 quizzes available count towards 12%

assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: During each Teams Skills and Smart Skills Workshop, you will complete an online quiz through Blackboard. The best 6 scores from 8 quizzes available will count towards the 12% that these quizzes contribute to your overall grade for the BIOM1001. Details of the quiz format and marking criteria will be communicated during the semester through Blackboard and by the Course Coordinators. This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Quizzes are completed online through Blackboard.
We recommend that you do not use the Blackboard Phone App nor a wireless network connection when completing this Blackboard assessment. Both are unstable and could affect your ability to complete your Blackboard assessment, by for example:
you may lose connectivity and lose the work you have completed; your submission could indicate you have completed your assessment task, but nothing has been delivered into Blackboard.
Please ensure that you only use a reliable internet connection.
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
No extensions are available for these quizzes unless under exceptional circumstances. Please contact the course coordinator to discuss your options.
Late submission
Late submission is not possible.

assessment_details.title: Biomedical Practice Exam
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited, Secure. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: The exam will cover content related to Module 1, including lectures, prescribed readings, and workshop content. The exam will consist of multiple-choice and various interactive questions. The exam will be a closed-book invigilated exam on campus, undertaken via the Inspera eAssessment platform. Students must bring a laptop that meets the device requirements for Inspera to the exam. It is essential to verify before the exam that your laptop meets the device requirements for using the Inspera Assessment platform. If you do not own a suitable laptop, you can borrow one from the Library . The Library website, "Get familiar with Inspera, " provides the latest information for students on using Inspera. Further information about the exam will be provided on Blackboard. Have your UQ student ID card available for all your exams. This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct . Read full task description Hide full task description

learning_resources.library_resources: Library resources are available on the UQ Library website .
learning_resources.other_course_materials:
Recommended Item Description Further Requirement Anatomy and Physiology 2e Anatomy and Physiology 2e is a freely available open access textbook that can be accessed at https://openstax.org/details/books/anatomy-and-physiology-2e

The book is organised by body systems and has a strong focus on the relationship between structure and function- a key concept in BIOM1001. Each section has review questions and critical thinking questions to aid your revision and study. Campbell Biology (published by Pearson, authors: Reece et al., 12th edition (Australia & NZ edition) This textbook is recommended and is also recommended for BIOL1020. Several copies will be available at UQ Library for on-site use. There is also an eBook available. The UQ license allows a limited number of students at a time to READ ONLINE. You can also download PDFs of each chapter. We recommend accessing this resource early in semester and downloading the relevant chapters for each module. It is important that you close your browser when you have finished reading so that other students can access the online option. Alternatively, digital copies of the textbook can be purchased via the Vital Source platform for around $50 for 6 months access or $65, for 12 months access to the textbook.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 3 (23 Feb - 15 Mar)
learning_activities.topic: Module 1 - Biomedical Practice [Suarez/Midwinter] Module 1 will be delivered by Associate Professor Rodrigo Suarez and Professor Mark Midwinter via 6 Lectures, one Team Skills Workshop, and one Smart Skills Workshop. This module will introduce you to Biomedical Science broadly, while introducing key topics including levels of biological organisation, systems anatomy/physiology, homeostasis in health (and disease), data appraisal, critical thinking and problem-solving. You will explore and apply your knowledge of these core concepts in other modules throughout the semester in the context of specific body systems. Learning outcomes: L01

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 10 (23 Feb - 10 May)
learning_activities.topic: Team Skills Workshops Team Skills Workshops will involve process-oriented, collaborative learning activities completed in teams, and an in-class quiz. There are four Team Skills Workshops across the semester: Week 1: Biomedical Practices Team Skills Workshop. Week 4: Nervous System Team Skills Workshop Week 8: Cardiovascular System Team Skills Workshop Week 11: Respiratory System Team Skills Workshop Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 2 To Week 11 (02 Mar - 17 May)
learning_activities.topic: Smart Skills Workshops Smart Skills Workshops will involve goal-oriented, collaborative learning activities completed in teams, and an in-class quiz. There are four Smart Skills Workshops across the semester: Week 2: Biomedical Practices Smart Skills Workshop Week 5: Nervous System Smart Skills Workshop Week 9: Cardiovascular System Smart Skills Workshop Week 12: Respiratory System Smart Skills Workshop Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 4 To Week 6 (16 Mar - 05 Apr)
learning_activities.topic: Module 2 - Nervous System [Coakley] Module 2 will be delivered by Dr Sean Coakley via 6 Lectures, one Team Skills Workshop, and one Smart Skills Workshop. In this module, you will be introduced to the structure and function of the nervous system. This incredibly complex network of billions of neurons controls every aspect of the human condition. You will learn about the cells that make up the nervous system, how their structure is related to their function and how they send electrical signals throughout the body to control every conscious and unconscious action we take. Learning outcomes: L01, L02

Biomed Cohort Connections Week Scheduled during the usual BIOM001 Lecture and Workshop times, this week of co-curricular activities will include fun and informative opportunities for you to engage with students in your cohort and across the Biomedical Science program, student societies, alumni, course teaching staff, and the wider biomed community at SBMS. Learning outcomes: L05, L06

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 8 To Week 10 (20 Apr - 10 May)
learning_activities.topic: Module 3 - Cardiovascular System [Thomas] Module 3 will be delivered by Professor Wally Thomas via 6 Lectures, one Team Skills Workshop, and one Smart Skills Workshop. In this module you will explore the physiology and anatomy of the cardiovascular system and the remarkable pump, the heart, that propels blood through our vessels. You will be introduced to how cardiac muscle contracts, how blood pressure is controlled and explore the huge burden of cardiovascular disease on our healthcare system. Learning outcomes: L01, L03

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 11 To Week 13 (11 May - 31 May)
learning_activities.topic: Module 4 - Respiratory System [Faber] Module 4 will be delivered by Dr Luli Faber via 6 Lectures, one Team Skills Workshop, and one Smart Skills Workshop. In this module you will explore why we breathe, what happens if we don't and the important jobs of our incredible respiratory system. Learning outcomes: L01, L04
